session_metadata.user_query: What is Elon Musk saying about Twitter recently?

moderately_effective.query: from:elonmusk (Twitter OR X OR "X.com" OR twitter.com)

most_effective_strategies.strategy: Graph Strategy: The investigation currently lacks the baseline dataset (Elon Musk's recent chronological posts), filtered hits for explicit mentions of Twitter/X, and up-to-date profile metadata and trend context. To fill those critical gaps efficiently and comply with the diversity requirement, the next round should pull the user's timeline, run a targeted Latest search for explicit references to Twitter/X, fetch profile metadata (which can show name/bio changes and verification/follower counts), check whether Twitter/X is trending in the United States, and retrieve follower information to gauge reach and influence. These endpoints complement one another: timeline gives the raw chronological corpus, search isolates Twitter/X mentions (including short posts and replies), profile and followers verify identity and amplification potential, and trends situates the visibility of the topic in the wider public conversation.

Graph Strategy: We need to quickly close the primary information gaps about what Elon Musk has been saying about Twitter/X in the most recent period. That requires: (1) collecting Elon Musk's raw posts (chronological) to see the full content and timing; (2) targeted searches that filter his posts for mentions of Twitter/X and related keywords so we don't miss brief references; (3) situational context (are Twitter/X topics trending) to understand reach and news hooks; and (4) follow-up conversation analysis on any high-impact tweet(s) to capture reactions and the narrative. To satisfy the investigation's diversity rule and the strategic imperative, this plan uses several complementary endpoints (timeline, targeted search, trends, profile verification, and conversation analysis) so the next round yields both breadth (all recent activity) and depth (threads and replies on key tweets). Some conversation endpoints are staged — they will be executed after step 1/2 identify specific tweet IDs.
